ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for sweet ayran. FOODON_03311737 (yogurt drink) is the closest parent category covering diluted yogurt-based beverages.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers fermented milk drinks broadly; no specific sweet ayran entry exists. Used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for ayran or sweet ayran as a distinct food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of sweet ayran is approximately 250 g (one glass), reflecting a typical restaurant or café portion.

nutrient_source.title: Yogurt, plain, whole milk (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #01116) adapted for sweet ayran formulation (yogurt diluted with water and sugar)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Sweet ayran is prepared by blending whole-milk yogurt with water (approximately 1:1 ratio) and adding sugar (~5–8 g per 100 ml). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy whole-milk yogurt (per 100 g: energy 61 kcal, protein 3.5 g, fat 3.3 g, carbohydrate 4.7 g) diluted by approximately 50% with water and adjusted for added sugar (~6 g per 100 g final beverage). Resulting estimates: energy ~52 kcal, protein ~1.8 g, fat ~1.6 g, carbohydrate ~7.5 g per 100 g. This is a medium-confidence adapted estimate.

gi_source.title: Yogurt, full-fat, plain — GI value 36 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 2008, International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sweet ayran. GI is estimated from full-fat plain yogurt (GI 36, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Sweet ayran is a diluted yogurt beverage with added sugar; the added sugar (~6 g per 100 g) slightly elevates the effective GI above plain yogurt. A conservative estimate of GI 36 is retained as the sugar content is modest and the dairy protein and fat matrix substantially blunts glycemic response. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (36 × 18.75 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 6.75, rounded to 6.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~40 mg per 100 g); dairy calcium may support blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: Sweet ayran is low in sodium (~100 mg per 250 g serving) and contains calcium from yogurt, making it a generally acceptable beverage for individuals managing hypertension, though added sugar should be monitored in the context of overall dietary sodium and caloric intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar (~6 g per 100 g) elevates carbohydrate load; low GI (36) from yogurt matrix partially mitigates glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sweet ayran contains added sugar that raises total carbohydrate to approximately 7.5 g per 100 g; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size and account for the ~18.75 g carbohydrate per 250 g serving within their daily carbohydrate budget, noting that the low GI (estimated 36) from the yogurt protein-fat matrix moderates the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.0 g per 100 g) from whole-milk yogurt base; low trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sweet ayran provides modest saturated fat (~2.5 g per 250 g serving) from its whole-milk yogurt base, which is within acceptable limits for most cardiovascular disease management plans; however, individuals on strict saturated fat restrictions should consider low-fat yogurt variants.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sweet ayran is a chilled, sweetened yogurt-based beverage of Turkish and Middle Eastern origin, prepared by blending whole-milk yogurt with water and adding sugar, and optionally a pinch of salt. Unlike traditional (savoury) ayran, the sweet variant omits or reduces salt and incorporates sugar to create a mildly sweet, tangy, creamy drink. The texture is smooth and slightly frothy when freshly blended. It is served cold and is popular as a refreshing accompaniment to spiced or grilled foods. In Singapore, sweet ayran appears on the menus of Indian-Mexican fusion restaurants, reflecting the cross-cultural culinary landscape of the city-state. Nutritionally, sweet ayran provides approximately 52 kcal per 100 g, with 1.8 g protein, 1.6 g fat, and 7.5 g carbohydrate (of which ~7 g is sugar from both lactose and added sugar). It is a source of calcium (~70 mg per 100 g) and riboflavin from the yogurt base. The glycemic index is estimated at 36 (low), as the protein and fat matrix of yogurt substantially blunts the glycemic response despite the added sugar. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, portion control is advised due to the added sugar content. The beverage is low in sodium and suitable for most hypertension management plans. It contains milk allergens and is not suitable for vegans or those with lactose intolerance.

confidence_reason: Sweet ayran is not listed as a distinct entry in any primary regional nutrition database (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, USDA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy whole-milk plain yogurt with a 50% water dilution and added sugar adjustment. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (full-fat plain yogurt, GI 36) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The cuisine tag in the input record is listed as Mexican, but sweet ayran is a Turkish/Middle Eastern beverage; the cuisine field has been corrected to Turkish/Middle Eastern. Medium confidence reflects the adapted nutrient estimation and analogous GI assignment.
notes: 1. The input record lists cuisine as 'Mexican', which is inconsistent with sweet ayran being a Turkish/Middle Eastern yogurt drink. The restaurant serving it (Fiesta Mexico - Three Amigos Indian and Mexican Restaurant) likely offers it as a cross-cultural menu item. The cuisine field has been corrected to 'Turkish / Middle Eastern' to reflect the food's true origin. 2. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA SR Legacy whole-milk yogurt diluted approximately 1:1 with water and adjusted for ~6 g added sugar per 100 g final beverage. These are medium-confidence estimates. 3. GI is estimated from full-fat plain yogurt (GI 36, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008) as the closest analogous food; no direct GI measurement for sweet ayran was found in approved sources. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as yogurt and water contain no dietary fibre; this is naturally zero for this beverage. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero; whole-milk yogurt contains negligible industrially produced trans fats. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this beverage formulation. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this beverage formulation. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this beverage formulation. 9. Multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided following Singaporean dish multilingual rules, as this food is served in Singapore. 10. glycemic_index set to 36 (low GI, analogous estimate from full-fat plain yogurt); gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (36 × 18.75) / 100 = 6.75, rounded to 6.8.
